_description: Sample: Exam Brief — NEET 2025, Academic Planner / Exam Analyst persona (cross-subject strategic summary)

response.exam_brief:
NEET 2025 STRATEGIC BRIEF — PRAJNA Intelligence (Exam Analyst View)

═══ MACRO SIGNALS ═══

PRAJNA SLM v3 analyzed 547 micro-topics across 3 subjects for NEET 2025.
Mean confidence: 0.74. High-confidence topics (≥0.80): 127 micro-topics.

• Biology: 90 marks (50% weight) | Composite importance: 0.87
  → Rising signal in Genetics (+0.12 YoY) and Biotechnology (+0.18 YoY)
  → Human Physiology holds at 0.85 — stable, high recurrence

• Chemistry: 45 marks (25%) | Composite importance: 0.73
  → Organic Chemistry rising (+0.09 YoY trend score)
  → Inorganic: stable but p-block elements anomaly detected (appears declining, -0.07)
  → Physical Chemistry: consistent, confidence 0.81

• Physics: 45 marks (25%) | Composite importance: 0.68
  → Mechanics: rising signal (+0.11)
  → Modern Physics: volatile — appeared 3/5 years at high weight, 2/5 at low
  → Optics: declining trend (-0.13) — lowest importance signal in 5 years

═══ ANOMALIES & RISK FLAGS ═══

1. Biotechnology & Its Applications: RISING FAST
   Signal: importance_prob jumped from 0.58 (2023) → 0.72 (2024) → 0.81 (2025)
   Risk: Many students and coaching materials under-index this chapter
   Recommendation: Upgrade to Tier A immediately

2. p-Block Elements: POTENTIAL DROP
   YoY signal: -0.07 | historical_freq: 0.70 (high) but recent trend declining
   Recommendation: Maintain Tier B, reduce allocation vs. 2024 strategy

3. Modern Physics (NEET): VOLATILITY WARNING
   topic_trend_score: -0.04 (nearly stable) but variance is high
   Confidence: 0.61 — PRAJNA SLM has lower certainty here
   Recommendation: Cover core topics (Photoelectric effect, Nuclear Physics) but do not over-invest

═══ PRIORITY TIER SUMMARY ═══

Tier A — Critical (combined expected marks: 108–118):
  Biology: Genetics, Human Physiology, Plant Physiology, Cell Biology
  Chemistry: Organic (Named Reactions, Biomolecules), Physical (Thermodynamics, Equilibrium)
  Physics: Mechanics, Electrostatics

Tier B — High ROI (combined: 45–55 marks):
  Biology: Ecology, Reproduction, Biotechnology
  Chemistry: Inorganic (coordination compounds, d-block)
  Physics: Modern Physics, Current Electricity

Tier C — Optional (<10 marks combined):
  Biology: The Living World, Animal Kingdom morphology
  Chemistry: Metallurgy, Environmental Chemistry
  Physics: Optics (geometrical), Communication Systems

═══ 60-DAY ALLOCATION MODEL ═══

Weeks 1–3 (Days 1–21):  Tier A Biology + Tier A Chemistry
Weeks 4–5 (Days 22–35): Tier A Physics + Tier B Biology
Weeks 6–7 (Days 36–49): Tier B Chemistry + Physics + Mock Tests
Week  8   (Days 50–60): Full revision + Weak-zone micro-topic drilling

═══ PREDICTION UNCERTAINTY ═══

Topics with confidence < 0.60 (interpret with caution):
  • Modern Physics (0.61), Communication Systems (0.55), Biotechnology Applications (0.63)
  PRAJNA recommendation: cover basics, avoid over-indexing on these for mark maximization

Generated by PRAJNA Intelligence v1.0 | Model: prajna-slm-v3 | Evidence: 547 micro-topics, 10-year exam data
